tluaMetatableGeneric.tlua(14,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaMetatableGeneric.tlua(25,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.


==== tluaMetatableGeneric.tlua (2 errors) ====
    // A pairing resolves its members eagerly, so it can only hold concrete types. A generic
    // table falls back to the intersection, which is less precise about collisions but does
    // instantiate -- the same trade a generic spread makes.
    
    local helpers = { describe = function(self: table): string return "d"; end };
    
    function withHelpers<T extends table>(value: T)
      return setmetatable(value, { __index = helpers });
    end
    
    local w = withHelpers({ own = "o" });
    local o1: string = w.own;
    local d1: string = w.describe(w);
    local wbad: number = w.own;
          ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
    // A concrete table whose *member* type mentions a type parameter still pairs, and the
    // pairing instantiates with it.
    function box<T>(value: T)
      return setmetatable({ value = value }, { __index = helpers });
    end
    
    local b = box(42);
    local v: number = b.value;
    local d2: string = b.describe(b);
    local bbad: string = b.value;
          ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
    
    local bs = box("s");
    local sv: string = bs.value;
